page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring in the top 25% at 2908.08. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall within the average tier, with a PassMark CPU score of 23917.02 and a PassMark memory score of 2894.0. The instance demonstrates solid execution in text generation for small language models, achieving 289.52 tokens per second, while medium-sized model inference and database operations perform at standard mid-tier levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC hardware profile offers a balanced resource density without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The lack of bundled local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 64.0 GB of system memory, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core capabilities and standard memory bandwidth. The instance is highly capable at small-model text generation and software compilation. Lacking local storage, it relies on network storage options, presenting a balanced resource-to-cost profile. It is best suited for general-purpose computing, software development, web serving, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and 64 GB RAM for reliable general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 64 GB of system memory (4 GB per vCPU)
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and general-purpose applications
